<!--
Thanks for contributing to PlugPulse! Please fill this out so reviewers
have the context they need. Keep PRs focused — one logical change per PR.
-->

## What does this PR do?

<!-- A clear, short description of the change and the motivation. -->

## Related issue

<!-- e.g. "Closes #42". PRs should usually map to an issue. -->
Closes #

## Type of change

- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that changes existing behavior)
- [ ] Docs / chore / CI

## How was this tested?

<!-- Commands you ran, screenshots for UI, or new tests added. -->

## Checklist

- [ ] My branch is up to date with `main`
- [ ] `ci` passes locally (backend: `ruff` + `mypy` + `pytest`; frontend: `npm run lint` + `npm run test`)
- [ ] I added/updated tests where it makes sense
- [ ] I updated docs where it makes sense
- [ ] I am not introducing proprietary dependencies or paid-API requirements into the core
- [ ] Any new data source attribution is preserved (see `ATTRIBUTION.md`)
- [ ] This change respects the performance budgets in `PERFORMANCE.md` (or I've noted the trade-off below)
